steps.title: Camera setup and environmental configuration See Appendix 1
steps.instruction: Camera setup and environmental configuration See Appendix 1 . Appendix 1. Three-view camera setup for multi-view recording of reach-and-grasp movements. The participant sat on a chair without a backrest, with the tested hand resting on the tabletop at the marked start position. The target object was placed on the body midline, 30 cm anterior to the participant. Camera A was positioned in front of the participant and aligned with the midsagittal plane. Cameras B and C were positioned at left and right oblique angles of approximately 45 degrees. The cameras were mounted on tripods at an approximate height of 120 cm and placed approximately 80 cm from the center of the tabletop measurement volume. Video was recorded at 1920 x 1080 pixels and 30 frames/s with fixed exposure and fixed focus. A checkerboard with 22 mm squares and 10 x 7 inner corners was used to calibrate the three-camera volume. This triangular camera configuration covered the tabletop movement area used for 3D reconstruction.

steps.title: Place Camera A frontal to the...
steps.instruction: Place Camera A frontal to the participant, aligned with the participant midsagittal plane on the opposite side of the table, and frame the full tabletop.

steps.title: Place Camera B at the left-anterior...
steps.instruction: Place Camera B at the left-anterior oblique position, approximately 45 degrees from the frontal view.

steps.title: Place Camera C at the right-anterior...
steps.instruction: Place Camera C at the right-anterior oblique position, approximately 45 degrees from the frontal view.

steps.title: Set each camera at approximately 120 cm height
steps.instruction: Set each camera at approximately 120 cm height. Configure all cameras to 1920 × 1080 resolution and 30 fps.

steps.title: Disable auto-exposure and autofocus when the...
steps.instruction: Disable auto-exposure and autofocus when the camera application allows manual control.

steps.title: Ask the participant to perform a practice reach-and-grasp task
steps.instruction: Ask the participant to perform a practice reach-and-grasp task. Use a real-time landmark overlay to confirm that all 21 hand landmarks remain trackable in all three views.

steps.title: Adjust camera angles until the hand...
steps.instruction: Adjust camera angles until the hand is visible from at least two cameras during object contact and lift. All cameras frame the table, start position, object position, and complete hand movement path. Landmark tracking remains stable during the practice task.

steps.title: Camera calibration Script
steps.instruction: Camera calibration Script: calibration.py

steps.title: Start recording on all three cameras
steps.instruction: Start recording on all three cameras.

steps.title: Move the checkerboard through the measurement...
steps.instruction: Move the checkerboard through the measurement volume for at least 30 seconds. Vary board orientation and depth while keeping the board fully visible in all three cameras.

steps.title: Stop recording and transfer calibration videos to the analysis workstation
steps.instruction: Stop recording and transfer calibration videos to the analysis workstation.

steps.title: Run the calibration script to estimate...
steps.instruction: Run the calibration script to estimate intrinsic parameters for each camera by checkerboard detection.

steps.title: Estimate pairwise extrinsic parameters between Camera...
steps.instruction: Estimate pairwise extrinsic parameters between Camera A-B and Camera A-C using stereo calibration with fixed intrinsics.

steps.title: Save intrinsics, distortion coefficients, extrinsics, and...
steps.instruction: Save intrinsics, distortion coefficients, extrinsics, and inter-camera baseline to a single calibration .npz file. Calibration.npz is generated and no camera pair has failed checkerboard detection across the calibration volume.

steps.title: Participant preparation
steps.instruction: Participant preparation

steps.title: Seat the participant without shoes at...
steps.instruction: Seat the participant without shoes at the edge of the 45 cm chair in upright unsupported sitting.

steps.title: Place the table anterior to the...
steps.instruction: Place the table anterior to the participant at 70 cm height.

steps.title: Set the start upper-limb posture as...
steps.instruction: Set the start upper-limb posture as shoulder neutral, elbow flexed to 90 degrees, forearm pronated, and hand resting on the tabletop.

steps.title: Mark the hand start position with tape
steps.instruction: Mark the hand start position with tape.

steps.title: Place the target object on the...
steps.instruction: Place the target object on the participant midline, 30 cm anterior to the start position.

steps.title: Mark the object position with tape for each condition
steps.instruction: Mark the object position with tape for each condition. The hand start position and target-object position are marked and used across all sets.

steps.title: Task recording and manual synchronization
steps.instruction: Task recording and manual synchronization

steps.title: Two operators start the three cameras on one verbal cue
steps.instruction: Two operators start the three cameras on one verbal cue. Perfect simultaneity is not required because the residual offset is corrected computationally.

steps.title: Instruct the participant
steps.instruction: Instruct the participant: From the start posture, reach forward to the target object, grasp it, lift it, return it to the marked position, and return your hand to the start position.

steps.title: Allow one practice trial
steps.instruction: Allow one practice trial.

steps.title: Record three consecutive reach-grasp-return cycles as one set
steps.instruction: Record three consecutive reach-grasp-return cycles as one set.

steps.title: Record two sets per object condition,...
steps.instruction: Record two sets per object condition, bottle and ball, on the affected and unaffected sides.

steps.title: Stop the cameras on a verbal...
steps.instruction: Stop the cameras on a verbal cue after the hand has returned to the start position at the end of the third cycle.

steps.title: Save each video with participant ID,...
steps.instruction: Save each video with participant ID, side, object condition, set number, and camera ID. Example: 01_Paretic_bottle_set1_CamA.MOV Each condition has synchronized video files from Camera A, Camera B, and Camera C with consistent filenames.

steps.title: 2D landmark extraction Script
steps.instruction: 2D landmark extraction Script: video_to_csv.py

steps.title: Load each camera video in Python
steps.instruction: Load each camera video in Python.

steps.title: Process each video frame by frame with MediaPipe Hands
steps.instruction: Process each video frame by frame with MediaPipe Hands.

steps.title: Extract the 21 hand landmarks for...
steps.instruction: Extract the 21 hand landmarks for each frame as 2D pixel coordinates and visibility values.

steps.title: Read frame time from the video...
steps.instruction: Read frame time from the video presentation timestamp using OpenCV CAP_PROP_POS_MSEC instead of assuming a fixed frame interval.

steps.title: Save per-camera CSV files containing frame...
steps.instruction: Save per-camera CSV files containing frame index, timestamp in seconds, 2D x and y pixel coordinates, and visibility for all 21 landmarks. Each camera produces a 2D landmark CSV with timestamps and 21 landmark coordinate pairs.

steps.title: Temporal synchronization across cameras Script
steps.instruction: Temporal synchronization across cameras Script: time_sync.py

steps.title: Load the three 2D landmark CSV files and calibration
steps.instruction: Load the three 2D landmark CSV files and calibration.npz.

steps.title: For each candidate temporal offset within...
steps.instruction: For each candidate temporal offset within the prespecified search window, perform provisional triangulation of landmarks.

steps.title: Back-project camera rays and compute minimum...
steps.instruction: Back-project camera rays and compute minimum inter-ray distance for each landmark and frame.

steps.title: Sum squared inter-ray distances across landmarks and frames
steps.instruction: Sum squared inter-ray distances across landmarks and frames.

steps.title: Select the offset that minimizes total squared inter-ray distance
steps.instruction: Select the offset that minimizes total squared inter-ray distance.

steps.title: Output SYNC_TIME_A, SYNC_TIME_B, and SYNC_TIME_C values...
steps.instruction: Output SYNC_TIME_A, SYNC_TIME_B, and SYNC_TIME_C values to the console; use these offsets as - sync-a, --sync-b, and --sync-c in data_integration.py. Synchronization offsets are printed and recorded in the analysis log.

steps.title: 3D reconstruction with dual-route output Script
steps.instruction: 3D reconstruction with dual-route output Script: data_integration.py

steps.title: Apply the synchronization offsets and resample...
steps.instruction: Apply the synchronization offsets and resample the per-camera 2D data to a uniform 30 fps grid by linear interpolation.

steps.title: For every frame and landmark, compute...
steps.instruction: For every frame and landmark, compute the primary three-camera 3D reconstruction from combination ABC and the two-camera fallback reconstructions from combinations AB, AC, and BC using DLT triangulation.

steps.title: Evaluate each DLT candidate by mean...
steps.instruction: Evaluate each DLT candidate by mean reprojection error across the available camera views.

steps.title: Screen each candidate by two constraints
steps.instruction: Screen each candidate by two constraints: index-finger proximal-phalanx length between 10.0 mm and 80.0 mm, and mean reprojection error below 10.0 mm. A camera subset is admitted to the candidate pool only when the index-finger metacarpophalangeal joint (landmark 5) is detected by every contributing camera, because this landmark is required (together with landmark 6, the index PIP joint) to evaluate the proximal-phalanx anatomical constraint. Because three-dimensional coordinates are not yet available at this stage, the pixel-to-millimetre conversion used by the reprojection-error gate is computed from an a priori working-distance estimate equal to 2.0 × the camera A–B baseline; the accuracy report in Step 8 uses the measured working distance Z̄ instead.

steps.title: Adopt the three-camera (ABC) candidate when it satisfies both constraints
steps.instruction: Adopt the three-camera (ABC) candidate when it satisfies both constraints. When the ABC candidate does not satisfy the constraints (for example, because one camera transiently fails to track the landmark due to self-occlusion), fall back to the two-camera combination (AB, AC, or BC) with the lowest reprojection error among those satisfying the two constraints. This hierarchical rule matches the sort key in the code — the number of contributing cameras is the primary sort key (descending) and the reprojection error is the secondary sort key (ascending).

steps.title: Classify frames for which neither the...
steps.instruction: Classify frames for which neither the three-camera nor any two-camera candidate satisfies the constraints as rescue frames. In the Raw dataset, retain the three-dimensional coordinate from the camera combination with the lowest reprojection error (regardless of whether it passed the constraint screen). In the Strict dataset, set these frames to NaN so that they are excluded from downstream segment-length and reliability analyses.

steps.title: Compute the valid-frame rate as the...
steps.instruction: Compute the valid-frame rate as the number of accepted frames divided by the total number of frames for each landmark. Save these values as Valid_Frame_Rate.csv and report them alongside downstream kinematic metrics.

steps.title: Output Route A
steps.instruction: Output Route A: Unfiltered_Raw.csv and Unfiltered_Strict.csv without any temporal filtering, for accuracy validation analyses in Step 8.

steps.title: Output Route B
steps.instruction: Output Route B: Raw.csv and Strict.csv after applying a fourth-order zero-lag Butterworth low-pass filter at 6.0 Hz, for kinematic and reliability analyses in Steps 11–12.

steps.title: Preserve NaN values in the Strict dataset during filtering
steps.instruction: Preserve NaN values in the Strict dataset during filtering. Do not interpolate rescue frames before filtering; if interpolation is required to run the filter, re-insert the NaN values at their original frame positions after filtering. Four 3D output files are generated (Unfiltered_Raw, Unfiltered_Strict, Raw, Strict) and Valid_Frame_Rate.csv is saved. Route A is reserved for validation, and Route B is reserved for kinematic and reliability analyses.

steps.title: Reprojection error validation Script
steps.instruction: Reprojection error validation Script: reprojection.py

steps.title: Load Unfiltered_Raw
steps.instruction: Load Unfiltered_Raw.csv from Route A and calibration.npz.

steps.title: Project each reconstructed 3D point back...
steps.instruction: Project each reconstructed 3D point back to the available camera image planes.

steps.title: Compute Euclidean pixel error between reprojected...
steps.instruction: Compute Euclidean pixel error between reprojected 2D coordinates and original MediaPipe 2D landmark coordinates.

steps.title: Convert pixel error to millimeters using...
steps.instruction: Convert pixel error to millimeters using a scaling factor derived from camera intrinsics and working distance estimated from 3D Z coordinates averaged over the wrist, index-finger MCP, and middle-finger MCP.

steps.title: Report mean and maximum reprojection error...
steps.instruction: Report mean and maximum reprojection error for each landmark in pixels and millimeters.

steps.title: Repeat the millimeter conversion using working-distance...
steps.instruction: Repeat the millimeter conversion using working-distance values varied by −30 % to +30 % (e.g., 0.70, 0.85, 1.00, 1.15, and 1.30 × the nominal value), and report the resulting sensitivity bounds. The output table contains mean error, maximum error, and −30 % to +30 % sensitivity bounds for each landmark.

steps.title: Anatomical consistency analysis Script
steps.instruction: Anatomical consistency analysis Script: anatomical_check.py

steps.title: Load Unfiltered_Raw
steps.instruction: Load Unfiltered_Raw.csv and Unfiltered_Strict.csv from Route A.

steps.title: Apply a fourth-order zero-lag 2
steps.instruction: Apply a fourth-order zero-lag 2.0 Hz Butterworth low-pass filter once on the unfiltered input. Preserve NaN values in the Strict dataset during filtering (do not interpolate NaN frames).

steps.title: Compute 3D Euclidean distance for each...
steps.instruction: Compute 3D Euclidean distance for each of 14 anatomical segment pairs.

steps.title: For each segment, compute mean length,...
steps.instruction: For each segment, compute mean length, standard deviation, and coefficient of variation, CV = SD / mean.

steps.title: Report the mean CV across all...
steps.instruction: Report the mean CV across all 14 segments as the global anatomical stability index. Each segment has mean length, SD, and CV for Raw and Strict reconstructions. The 14-segment mean CV is reported.

steps.title: Single-camera comparison Script
steps.instruction: Single-camera comparison Script: single_anatomical_check.py

steps.title: Extract 2D landmark data from Camera...
steps.instruction: Extract 2D landmark data from Camera A only and treat it as the monocular input.

steps.title: Apply the same 2
steps.instruction: Apply the same 2.0 Hz low-pass filtering and compute segment-length metrics for the same 14 anatomical pairs.

steps.title: Compare the 14-segment mean CV from...
steps.instruction: Compare the 14-segment mean CV from the monocular condition with the three-camera (ABC-primary) Strict dataset from Step 9.

steps.title: Report the comparison as the gain...
steps.instruction: Report the comparison as the gain in anatomical stability attributable to three-camera reconstruction with two-camera fallback under occlusion. Monocular and three-camera-primary Strict mean CV values are reported on the same scale.

steps.title: Database construction for reliability analysis Script
steps.instruction: Database construction for reliability analysis Script: database.py

steps.title: For each participant and each condition,...
steps.instruction: For each participant and each condition, load Trial 1 and Trial 2 from the 6.0 Hz filtered Strict dataset in Route B.

steps.title: Compute per-frame lengths for the same 14 anatomical segment pairs
steps.instruction: Compute per-frame lengths for the same 14 anatomical segment pairs.

steps.title: Compute the trial-wise temporal mean length...
steps.instruction: Compute the trial-wise temporal mean length in millimeters for each segment, excluding NaN frames.

steps.title: Append one row per participant, condition, and segment to ICC_Database_14Pairs
steps.instruction: Append one row per participant, condition, and segment to ICC_Database_14Pairs.csv, with Trial 1 and Trial 2 mean values. Use case-consistent condition labels (for example, "Paretic_Bottle" only; avoid case sensitive duplicates such as "PARETIC_Bottle" and "Paretic_Bottle" in the same database). If incremental updates have already produced case-sensitive duplicates, normalise condition labels to a single canonical Title-case form and retain the first occurrence of each (subject, condition, segment) row. The reliability analysis in Step 12 pools (subject × condition) pairs as the unit of observation; in the pilot validation set this yields 2 participants × 2 conditions = 4 paired observations per segment. ICC_Database_14Pairs.csv contains SubjectID, Condition, Segment, Set1_Mean, and Set2_Mean, with no case-sensitive duplicate condition labels.

steps.title: Reliability, measurement error, and minimal detectable change Script: icc
steps.instruction: Reliability, measurement error, and minimal detectable change Script: icc.py

steps.title: Load ICC_Database_14Pairs
steps.instruction: Load ICC_Database_14Pairs.csv.

steps.title: For each segment, compute the two-way...
steps.instruction: For each segment, compute the two-way random-effects, absolute-agreement, single-measurement intraclass correlation coefficient, ICC (2,1), using pingouin.

steps.title: Compute standard error of measurement as...
steps.instruction: Compute standard error of measurement as SEM = SD × sqrt(1 - ICC).

steps.title: Compute minimal detectable change at 95...
steps.instruction: Compute minimal detectable change at 95 % confidence as MDC95 = 1.96 × sqrt(2) × SEM.

steps.title: Report ICC (2,1), 95 % confidence...
steps.instruction: Report ICC (2,1), 95 % confidence interval, SEM, and MDC95 for all 14 segments. The reliability summary table contains ICC (2,1), 95 % CI, SEM, and MDC95 for each segment.
